mapper.select: select name, lock_until, locked_at, locked_by from batch.shedlock order by name
select delivery_status, count(*) as cnt from batch.event_delivery_log where tenant_id = #{tenantId} group by delivery_status
select count(*) from batch.outbox_event where tenant_id = #{tenantId} and publish_status = 'NEW'

select count(1) from batch.worker_registry where tenant_id = #{tenantId} and status = 'ONLINE' and ( heartbeat_at is null or heartbeat_at < current_timestamp - (#{timeoutSeconds} * interval '1 second') )
select count(1) from batch.worker_registry where tenant_id = #{tenantId} and status = 'DRAINING' and drain_deadline_at is not null and drain_deadline_at < current_timestamp

select count(distinct wr.id) from batch.worker_registry wr join batch.job_task jt on jt.tenant_id = wr.tenant_id and jt.assigned_worker_code = wr.worker_code where wr.tenant_id = #{tenantId} and wr.status = 'DECOMMISSIONED' and jt.task_status in ('CREATED', 'READY', 'RUNNING')

select count(1) from batch.worker_registry where tenant_id = #{tenantId} and status in ('ONLINE', 'DRAINING') and capability_tags is not null and ( case when jsonb_typeof(capability_tags) = 'array' then exists ( select 1 from jsonb_array_elements(capability_tags) elem where jsonb_typeof(elem) <> 'string' ) else true end )

select id as "id", tenant_id as "tenantId", instance_no as "instanceNo", job_code as "jobCode", biz_date as "bizDate", instance_status as "instanceStatus", queue_code as "queueCode", worker_group as "workerGroup", trace_id as "traceId", started_at as "startedAt", deadline_at as "deadlineAt" from batch.job_instance where tenant_id = #{tenantId} and id = #{jobInstanceId}

select count(distinct ji.id) from batch.job_instance ji where ji.tenant_id = #{tenantId} and ji.instance_status in ('SUCCESS', 'FAILED', 'PARTIAL_FAILED', 'CANCELLED', 'TERMINATED') and ( exists ( select 1 from batch.job_partition p where p.tenant_id = ji.tenant_id and p.job_instance_id = ji.id and p.partition_status in ('CREATED', 'WAITING', 'READY', 'RUNNING', 'RETRYING') ) or exists ( select 1 from batch.job_task t where t.tenant_id = ji.tenant_id and t.job_instance_id = ji.id and t.task_status in ('CREATED', 'READY', 'RUNNING') ) )

select worker_group as "workerGroup", count(1) as "totalWorkers", count(*) filter (where status = 'ONLINE') as "onlineWorkers", count(*) filter (where status = 'DRAINING') as "drainingWorkers", count(*) filter (where status = 'OFFLINE') as "offlineWorkers", count(*) filter (where status = 'DECOMMISSIONED') as "decommissionedWorkers", coalesce(sum(current_load) filter (where status = 'ONLINE'), 0) as "currentLoad", coalesce(sum(max_concurrent) filter (where status = 'ONLINE'), 0) as "maxConcurrent" from batch.worker_registry where tenant_id = #{tenantId} group by worker_group order by worker_group

select partition_status as "status", count(1) as "count" from batch.job_partition where tenant_id = #{tenantId} and job_instance_id = #{jobInstanceId} group by partition_status order by partition_status
select task_status as "status", count(1) as "count" from batch.job_task where tenant_id = #{tenantId} and job_instance_id = #{jobInstanceId} group by task_status order by task_status

select jt.id as "taskId", jt.job_partition_id as "jobPartitionId", jt.task_status as "taskStatus", jt.assigned_worker_code as "assignedWorkerCode", jt.heartbeat_at as "heartbeatAt", wr.status as "workerStatus", wr.heartbeat_at as "workerHeartbeatAt", case when jt.assigned_worker_code is null then 'TASK_NOT_ASSIGNED' when wr.id is null then 'WORKER_NOT_REGISTERED' when wr.status not in ('ONLINE', 'DRAINING') then 'WORKER_NOT_AVAILABLE' when jt.task_status = 'RUNNING' and (jt.heartbeat_at is null or jt.heartbeat_at < current_timestamp - (#{heartbeatTimeoutSeconds} * interval '1 second')) then 'RUNNING_TASK_HEARTBEAT_STALE' else null end as "reasonCode" from batch.job_task jt left join batch.worker_registry wr on wr.tenant_id = jt.tenant_id and wr.worker_code = jt.assigned_worker_code where jt.tenant_id = #{tenantId} and jt.job_instance_id = #{jobInstanceId} and jt.task_status in ('CREATED', 'READY', 'RUNNING') and ( jt.assigned_worker_code is null or wr.id is null or wr.status not in ('ONLINE', 'DRAINING') or ( jt.task_status = 'RUNNING' and ( jt.heartbeat_at is null or jt.heartbeat_at < current_timestamp - (#{heartbeatTimeoutSeconds} * interval '1 second') ) ) ) order by jt.id limit 20

select oe.publish_status as "status", count(1) as "count", min(oe.created_at) as "oldest", max(oe.updated_at) as "newest" from batch.outbox_event oe join batch.job_task jt on jt.tenant_id = oe.tenant_id and jt.id = oe.aggregate_id where oe.tenant_id = #{tenantId} and oe.aggregate_type = 'JOB_TASK' and jt.job_instance_id = #{jobInstanceId} group by publish_status order by publish_status
